tEn: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
tZh: 越狱：LLM安全训练为何失败？
sEn: Safety training fails due to competing objectives and mismatched generalization, enabling successful jailbreak attacks even on state-of-the-art models.
sZh: 安全训练因竞争目标和错配泛化而失败，使得越狱攻击即使在最先进的模型上也能成功。
absEn: Large language models trained for safety and harmlessness remain susceptible to adversarial misuse, as evidenced by the prevalence of "jailbreak" attacks on early releases of ChatGPT that elicit undesired behavior. Going beyond recognition of the issue, we investigate why such attacks succeed and how they can be created. We hypothesize two failure modes of safety training: competing objectives and mismatched generalization. Competing objectives arise when a model's capabilities and safety goals conflict, while mismatched generalization occurs when safety training fails to generalize to a domain for which capabilities exist. We use these failure modes to guide jailbreak design and then evaluate state-of-the-art models, including OpenAI's GPT-4 and Anthropic's Claude v1.3, against both existing and newly designed attacks. We find that vulnerabilities persist despite the extensive red-teaming and safety-training efforts behind these models. Notably, new attacks utilizing our failure modes succeed on every prompt in a collection of unsafe requests from the models' red-teaming evaluation sets and outperform existing ad hoc jailbreaks. Our analysis emphasizes the need for safety-capability parity -- that safety mechanisms should be as sophisticated as the underlying model -- and argues against the idea that scaling alone can resolve these safety failure modes.
absZh: 经过安全和无害性训练的大型语言模型仍然容易受到对抗性滥用，早期发布的ChatGPT上普遍存在的“越狱”攻击就是明证，这些攻击会引发不良行为。在认识到问题的基础上，我们研究了这些攻击为何成功以及如何创建它们。我们假设了安全训练的两种失败模式：竞争目标和错配泛化。竞争目标出现在模型的能力和安全目标冲突时，而错配泛化则发生在安全训练未能泛化到存在能力的领域时。我们利用这些失败模式指导越狱设计，然后评估了最先进的模型，包括OpenAI的GPT-4和Anthropic的Claude v1.3，针对现有和新设计的攻击。我们发现，尽管这些模型背后有广泛的红队测试和安全训练努力，但漏洞仍然存在。值得注意的是，利用我们失败模式的新攻击在模型红队测试评估集中的每个不安全请求提示上都成功了，并且优于现有的临时越狱方法。我们的分析强调了安全-能力对等性的必要性——安全机制应与底层模型一样复杂——并反对仅靠扩展就能解决这些安全失败模式的观点。

contrib.en: Identifies two failure modes of LLM safety training: competing objectives and mismatched generalization.
contrib.zh: 识别出大语言模型安全训练的两个失败模式：竞争目标和错配泛化。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that these failure modes can be exploited to create effective jailbreak attacks.
contrib.zh: 证明这些失败模式可被利用来创建有效的越狱攻击。

contrib.en: Shows that state-of-the-art models like GPT-4 and Claude v1.3 remain vulnerable to jailbreaks.
contrib.zh: 表明GPT-4和Claude v1.3等最先进模型仍然容易受到越狱攻击。

contrib.en: Proposes safety-capability parity: safety mechanisms must be as sophisticated as the underlying model.
contrib.zh: 提出安全-能力对等：安全机制必须与底层模型一样复杂。

contrib.en: Argues that scaling alone cannot resolve these safety failure modes.
contrib.zh: 论证仅靠Scaling（规模扩张）无法解决这些安全失败模式。

limits.en: Limited to black-box access; white-box attacks may reveal additional vulnerabilities.
limits.zh: 仅限于黑盒访问；白盒攻击可能揭示更多漏洞。

limits.en: Evaluation only on English prompts; generalization to other languages is untested.
limits.zh: 仅对英文提示进行评估；对其他语言的泛化性未经测试。

limits.en: Attacks may not transfer to models with different training paradigms or architectures.
limits.zh: 攻击可能无法迁移到具有不同训练范式或架构的模型。

limits.en: Study focuses on refusal bypass; other safety aspects like bias or toxicity are not addressed.
limits.zh: 研究侧重于绕过拒绝机制；未涉及偏见或毒性等其他安全问题。

limits.en: Proprietary models limit reproducibility and direct hypothesis confirmation.
limits.zh: 专有模型限制了可重复性和直接假设验证。

paras.en: Large language models trained for safety and harmlessness remain susceptible to adversarial misuse, as evidenced by the prevalence of “jailbreak” attacks on early releases of ChatGPT that elicit undesired behavior. Going beyond recognition of the issue, we investigate why such attacks succeed and how they can be created. We hypothesize two failure modes of safety training: competing objectives and mismatched generalization. Competing objectives arise when a model’s capabilities and safety goals conflict, while mismatched generalization occurs when safety training fails to generalize to a domain for which capabilities exist. We use these failure modes to guide jailbreak design and then evaluate state-of-the-art models, including OpenAI’s GPT-4 and Anthropic’s Claude v1.3, against both existing and newly designed attacks. We find that vulnerabilities persist despite the extensive red-teaming and safety-training efforts behind these models. Notably, new attacks utilizing our failure modes succeed on every prompt in a collection of unsafe requests from the models’ red-teaming evaluation sets and outperform existing ad hoc jailbreaks. Our analysis emphasizes the need for safety-capability parity—that safety mechanisms should be as sophisticated as the underlying model—and argues against the idea that scaling alone can resolve these safety failure modes.
paras.zh: 经过安全性和无害性训练的大型语言模型仍然容易受到对抗性滥用，早期发布的ChatGPT上普遍存在的“越狱”攻击引发了不良行为，这证明了这一点。除了认识到这个问题，我们研究了这些攻击为何成功以及如何创建它们。我们假设安全训练存在两种失败模式：竞争目标和泛化不匹配。当模型的能力和安全目标发生冲突时，会出现竞争目标；而当安全训练未能泛化到能力存在的领域时，则会出现泛化不匹配。我们利用这些失败模式来指导越狱设计，然后评估最先进的模型，包括OpenAI的GPT-4和Anthropic的Claude v1.3，针对现有和新设计的攻击。我们发现，尽管这些模型背后有广泛的红队测试和安全训练努力，但漏洞仍然存在。值得注意的是，利用我们失败模式的新攻击在模型红队测试评估集中的一组不安全请求的每个提示上都成功，并且优于现有的临时越狱方法。我们的分析强调了安全-能力对等性的必要性——安全机制应与底层模型一样复杂——并反对仅靠Scaling（规模扩张）就能解决这些安全失败模式的观点。

paras.en: In recent months, large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, Claude, and Bard have seen widespread deployment. These models exhibit advanced general capabilities [38], but also pose risks around misuse by bad actors (e.g., for misinformation or for crime [9, 32, 25, 30, 28]).
paras.zh: 近几个月来，ChatGPT、Claude 和 Bard 等大型语言模型（LLM）得到了广泛部署。这些模型展现出先进的通用能力 [38]，但也带来了被恶意行为者滥用的风险（例如，用于传播错误信息或犯罪 [9, 32, 25, 30, 28]）。

paras.en: To mitigate these risks of misuse, model creators have implemented safety mechanisms to restrict model behavior to a “safe” subset of capabilities. These include both training-time interventions to align models with predefined values [41, 7] and post hoc flagging and filtering of inputs and outputs [56, 24, 52, 45]. These efforts are often complemented by red teaming, which proactively identifies and trains against weaknesses [42, 23, 38].
paras.zh: 为了减轻这些滥用风险，模型创建者实施了安全机制，将模型行为限制在“安全”的能力子集内。这些机制包括训练时的干预措施，以使模型与预定义价值观对齐 [41, 7]，以及事后对输入和输出进行标记和过滤 [56, 24, 52, 45]。这些工作通常辅以红队测试，主动识别并针对弱点进行训练 [42, 23, 38]。

paras.en: While hardening LLMs for safety can help [38], models remain vulnerable to adversarial inputs, as demonstrated by the spread of “jailbreaks” for ChatGPT on social media since its initial release [13, 17, 2]. These attacks are engineered to elicit behavior, such as producing harmful content or leaking personally identifiable information, that the model was trained to avoid. Attacks can range from elaborate role play (e.g., DAN [48]) to subtle subversion of the safety objective (see Figure 1(a)). Model creators have acknowledged and updated their models against jailbreak attacks [7, 38, 10, 5], but a systematic analysis and a conceptual understanding of this phenomenon remains lacking.
paras.zh: 尽管对 LLM 进行安全加固有所帮助 [38]，但模型仍然容易受到对抗性输入的攻击，自 ChatGPT 首次发布以来，社交媒体上“越狱”方法的传播就证明了这一点 [13, 17, 2]。这些攻击旨在诱导模型产生其被训练避免的行为，例如生成有害内容或泄露个人身份信息。攻击手段从精心设计的角色扮演（例如 DAN [48]）到对安全目标的微妙颠覆（见图 1(a)）。模型创建者已承认并更新了模型以抵御越狱攻击 [7, 38, 10, 5]，但对此现象的系统分析和概念理解仍然缺乏。

paras.en: In this work, we analyze the vulnerability of safety-trained LLMs to jailbreak attacks by examining the model’s pretraining and safety training processes. Based on known safety training methods, we hypothesize two failure modes—competing objectives and mismatched generalization—that shed light on why jailbreaks exist and enable the creation of new attacks. This understanding suggests that jailbreaks, rather than being isolated phenomena, are inherent to how models are currently trained.
paras.zh: 在这项工作中，我们通过检查模型的预训练和安全训练过程，分析了经过安全训练的 LLM 对越狱攻击的脆弱性。基于已知的安全训练方法，我们假设了两种失败模式——竞争目标和错配泛化——这揭示了越狱存在的原因，并使得创建新攻击成为可能。这种理解表明，越狱并非孤立现象，而是当前模型训练方式固有的问题。

paras.en: In more detail, competing objectives occur when a model’s pretraining and instruction-following objectives are put at odds with its safety objective (Figure 1(a)). In contrast, mismatched generalization arises when inputs are out-of-distribution for a model’s safety training data but within the scope of its broad pretraining corpus (Figure 1(b)). We use these two principles to guide our exploration of the design space of attacks, with each principle alone yielding a variety of individual attacks.
paras.zh: 更详细地说，竞争目标发生在模型的预训练和指令遵循目标与其安全目标相冲突时（图 1(a)）。相反，错配泛化出现在输入对于模型的安全训练数据是分布外，但在其广泛的预训练语料库范围内时（图 1(b)）。我们利用这两个原理来指导我们对攻击设计空间的探索，每个原理单独就能产生多种不同的攻击。

paras.en: We then conduct an empirical evaluation of state-of-the-art safety-trained models, including OpenAI’s GPT-4 and Anthropic’s Claude v1.3, against both existing and newly constructed jailbreak attacks. We evaluate on both a curated dataset of harmful prompts from these models’ red-teaming evaluation sets and a larger synthetic dataset of harmful prompts for broader coverage. Despite extensive safety training—including updating against jailbreak attacks since the models’ initial releases [10, 5]—we find that the models remain vulnerable. Attacks based on our two principles outperform existing ad hoc jailbreaks and succeed on over 96% of the evaluated prompts, including on 100% of the curated red-teaming prompts that past safety interventions were designed to address.
paras.zh: 然后，我们对最先进的安全训练模型（包括 OpenAI 的 GPT-4 和 Anthropic 的 Claude v1.3）进行了实证评估，针对现有和新构建的越狱攻击。我们使用来自这些模型红队测试评估集的 curated 有害提示数据集，以及一个更大的合成有害提示数据集以扩大覆盖范围。尽管进行了广泛的安全训练——包括自模型首次发布以来针对越狱攻击的更新 [10, 5]——我们发现模型仍然脆弱。基于我们两个原理的攻击优于现有的临时越狱方法，并在超过 96% 的评估提示上成功，包括在 100% 的 curated 红队测试提示上成功，而这些提示正是过去安全干预措施旨在解决的。

paras.en: Finally, we analyze defense. Combining our analysis of failure modes with our empirical study, we argue that jailbreaks may be inherent to existing safety training methods. Scaling up will not resolve competing objectives, as the issue lies with the optimization objective, and may even exacerbate mismatched generalization if safety training is not suitably extended to broader domains. Moreover, our findings suggest the necessity of safety-capability parity—safety mechanisms should be as sophisticated as the underlying model. Otherwise, attacks will exploit cutting-edge capabilities of the underlying model that less sophisticated safety mechanisms cannot detect.
paras.zh: 最后，我们分析了防御。结合我们对失败模式的分析和实证研究，我们认为越狱可能是现有安全训练方法固有的问题。Scaling（规模扩张）不会解决竞争目标，因为问题在于优化目标，甚至可能加剧错配泛化，如果安全训练没有适当地扩展到更广泛的领域。此外，我们的发现表明安全-能力对等性的必要性——安全机制应与底层模型一样复杂。否则，攻击将利用底层模型的前沿能力，而较不复杂的安全机制无法检测到。

paras.en: By highlighting failure modes and limitations of existing methods to align LLMs for safety, we hope to inspire further discussion and analysis around the responsible development and deployment of such models. As LLMs become more capable and widely used, the need for informed assessments of model safety, including in adversarial contexts, only becomes more urgent. We thus view an open dialogue on vulnerabilities and limitations of existing methods as a step towards this goal.
paras.zh: 通过强调现有方法在使 LLM 安全对齐方面的失败模式和局限性，我们希望激发围绕此类模型负责任开发和部署的进一步讨论和分析。随着 LLM 变得更有能力并被广泛使用，对模型安全性（包括对抗性环境）进行知情评估的需求只会变得更加紧迫。因此，我们将关于现有方法脆弱性和局限性的公开对话视为朝着这一目标迈出的一步。

paras.en: We communicated preliminary results to OpenAI and Anthropic and have received their acknowledgment of this work. To increase barriers to misuse of the discussed attacks while the issues we highlight are resolved, we omit specific prompts for the strongest attacks and focus on describing their construction in conceptual terms. We discuss ethical considerations and responsible disclosure norms further in Section 6.
paras.zh: 我们已将初步结果告知 OpenAI 和 Anthropic，并已收到他们对此工作的确认。为了在我们强调的问题得到解决之前增加所讨论攻击的滥用门槛，我们省略了最强攻击的具体提示，并专注于从概念上描述其构建。我们在第 6 节进一步讨论伦理考量和负责任的披露规范。

paras.en: Concerns about the growing capabilities of AI models have led to the development of models aligned with human values, as increased capabilities correspond to heightened opportunities for misuse and harm [24, 52, 45, 9, 32, 25]. Safety training methods for LLMs, such as GPT-4 and Claude, typically finetune pretrained models using human preferences [18, 58, 46, 41, 6] and AI feedback [7, 38, 47]. These methods can be used alongside filtering [52, 50, 38] and scrubbing the training data [40, 34].
paras.zh: 对AI模型能力日益增长的担忧促使了与人类价值观对齐的模型的开发，因为能力的提升对应着滥用和危害风险的增加[24, 52, 45, 9, 32, 25]。针对GPT-4和Claude等大语言模型的安全训练方法通常使用人类偏好[18, 58, 46, 41, 6]和AI反馈[7, 38, 47]对预训练模型进行微调。这些方法可与过滤[52, 50, 38]和清洗训练数据[40, 34]结合使用。

paras.en: The susceptibility of LLMs (without safety interventions) to adversarial interactions has been explored in the contexts of red teaming [42, 23], extracting training data [14, 34], and adversarial prompting [49, 29], among others. For safety-trained language models, recent works have studied the potential of extracting harmful behavior [23, 30, 26, 33, 55, 28, 20]. Most closely related are Kang et al. [30], who study attacking GPT-3.5 via a computer security lens, and Li et al. [33], who focus on personally identifiable information (PII) extraction rather than general harm. However, neither pursues our goal of understanding jailbreaks from a conceptual point of view. Beyond research papers, jailbreaks have also received widespread attention in online discourse and the media [13, 17, 21, 22, 54, 27, 1, 19, 35], with many attacks being discovered and shared in a decentralized manner.
paras.zh: 大语言模型（未进行安全干预）对对抗性交互的敏感性已在红队测试[42, 23]、提取训练数据[14, 34]和对抗性提示[49, 29]等背景下得到探索。对于经过安全训练的语言模型，近期工作研究了提取有害行为的可能性[23, 30, 26, 33, 55, 28, 20]。最相关的是Kang等人[30]从计算机安全角度研究攻击GPT-3.5，以及Li等人[33]专注于个人可识别信息（PII）提取而非一般危害。然而，两者均未追求我们从概念角度理解越狱的目标。除研究论文外，越狱在在线讨论和媒体中也受到广泛关注[13, 17, 21, 22, 54, 27, 1, 19, 35]，许多攻击以去中心化方式被发现和分享。

paras.en: There also exists an extensive literature on adversarial examples for deep learning models in natural language processing and elsewhere (see Chakraborty et al. [15] and Zhang et al. [57] for surveys). A key distinction between these works and our setting is that jailbreak attacks aim to elicit unsafe capabilities rather than cause model errors. Additionally, unlike much of this literature, jailbreak attacks can be constructed in an input-agnostic way and tend to be human-interpretable in nature.
paras.zh: 在自然语言处理及其他领域，深度学习模型的对抗样本已有大量文献（参见Chakraborty等人[15]和Zhang等人[57]的综述）。这些工作与我们的设置之间的一个关键区别在于，越狱攻击旨在引发不安全能力而非导致模型错误。此外，与这些文献中的许多工作不同，越狱攻击可以以输入无关的方式构建，并且本质上具有人类可解释性。

full.sec: 2 Background: Safety-Trained Language Models and Jailbreak Attacks
full.secZh: 2 背景：安全训练的语言模型与越狱攻击

paras.en: In this section, we provide an overview of safety-trained language models and their vulnerability to jailbreak attacks. We first introduce safety training and a threat model of attacks. We then discuss the models and datasets we use to evaluate the vulnerability landscape of safety-trained language models.
paras.zh: 在本节中，我们概述了安全训练的语言模型及其对越狱攻击的脆弱性。我们首先介绍安全训练和攻击的威胁模型。然后讨论用于评估安全训练语言模型脆弱性状况的模型和数据集。

paras.en: To mitigate potential harm and misuse, language models are often trained to refuse certain categories of prompts during safety training. For example, GPT-4 and Claude were both trained to refuse (see Figure 1) requests for harmful information [38, 23]. We thus define restricted behaviors as behaviors that a safety-trained language model is trained to avoid. Restricted behaviors are often harmful, such as creating misinformation or aiding crime (e.g., the prompt in Figure 1 asks for help with vandalism). Other examples can include leaking personally identifiable information and hallucinating personal details about the model. For our experiments, to be sure that we are attempting to elicit restricted behaviors, we start with prompts deemed unsafe in red-teaming evaluations by the model creators.
paras.zh: 为了减轻潜在危害和滥用，语言模型在安全训练中通常被训练为拒绝某些类别的提示。例如，GPT-4 和 Claude 都被训练为拒绝（见图 1）有害信息的请求 [38, 23]。因此，我们将受限行为定义为经过安全训练的语言模型被训练避免的行为。受限行为通常是有害的，例如制造错误信息或协助犯罪（例如，图 1 中的提示请求帮助破坏公物）。其他示例可能包括泄露个人身份信息和幻觉化关于模型的个人细节。在我们的实验中，为了确保我们试图引发受限行为，我们从模型创建者在红队评估中认为不安全的提示开始。

paras.en: A jailbreak attack on a safety-trained model is an attempt to elicit an on-topic response to a prompt P𝑃P for restricted behavior by submitting a modified prompt P′superscript𝑃′P^{\prime}. We let the attacker have black-box access to the model (i.e., they cannot see model weights or training data) through a chat interface, but do not let them modify the system prompt or alter message history. Attacks can be adaptive, in that the attacker can select P′superscript𝑃′P^{\prime} based on model responses to other variations of P𝑃P, although most of our attacks succeed even without adaptivity. Figure 1 shows examples of successful attacks, where P𝑃P is “What tools do I need to cut down a stop sign?”.
paras.zh: 对安全训练模型的越狱攻击是尝试通过提交修改后的提示 P′ 来引发对受限行为提示 P 的切题响应。我们允许攻击者通过聊天界面具有对模型的黑盒访问权限（即，他们无法看到模型权重或训练数据），但不允许他们修改系统提示或更改消息历史。攻击可以是自适应的，即攻击者可以根据模型对 P 的其他变体的响应来选择 P′，尽管我们的大多数攻击即使没有自适应性也能成功。图 1 显示了成功攻击的示例，其中 P 是“我需要什么工具来砍掉一个停车标志？”。

paras.en: To evaluate the success of a jailbreak attack, we define three classes of outcomes for a given prompt P𝑃P and attack P′superscript𝑃′P^{\prime}. An outcome is labeled “Good Bot\xspace” if the model refuses P′superscript𝑃′P^{\prime}, “Bad Bot\xspace” if the model responds to P′superscript𝑃′P^{\prime} with an on-topic response to P𝑃P, and “Unclear\xspace” otherwise.111We use Good Bot\xspace and Bad Bot\xspace rather than “success” or “failure” to disambiguate between successful attacks and successful defenses. See Appendix B for further details on the labeling scheme. An outcome can be unclear if the model does not understand P′superscript𝑃′P^{\prime} or if its response is not on topic for P𝑃P (e.g., describing how to make a bath bomb when asked how to make a bomb). We note this evaluation scheme does not score on-topic responses on quality or accuracy, as our focus is on bypassing refusal mechanisms. Anecdotally, however, jailbroken responses often appear informative and accurate.
paras.zh: 为了评估越狱攻击的成功，我们为给定提示 P 和攻击 P′ 定义了三类结果。如果模型拒绝 P′，则结果标记为“好机器人”；如果模型对 P′ 给出对 P 的切题响应，则标记为“坏机器人”；否则标记为“不明确”。111我们使用“好机器人”和“坏机器人”而不是“成功”或“失败”，以区分成功的攻击和成功的防御。有关标记方案的更多细节，请参见附录 B。如果模型不理解 P′ 或其响应与 P 不相关（例如，当被问及如何制造炸弹时，描述如何制作浴盐球），则结果可能不明确。我们注意到，这种评估方案不评估切题响应的质量或准确性，因为我们的重点是绕过拒绝机制。然而，根据经验，越狱后的响应通常看起来信息丰富且准确。

full.secZh: 2.2 评估安全训练的语言模型

paras.en: To assess the vulnerability of safety-trained LLMs to jailbreaks, we test state-of-the-art models from OpenAI and Anthropic on two datasets of harmful requests: a curated set of 32 prompts adapted from red-teaming efforts of OpenAI and Anthropic [38, 7] and a held-out set of 317 prompts (that was not seen by the authors until after data collection).
paras.zh: 为了评估安全训练的大语言模型对越狱攻击的脆弱性，我们测试了来自 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的最先进模型，使用两个有害请求数据集：一个由 32 个提示组成的精选集，改编自 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的红队测试工作 [38, 7]；以及一个包含 317 个提示的保留集（作者在数据收集之前未见过该数据集）。

paras.en: We evaluate language models that are state-of-the-art in both capabilities and safety. From OpenAI, we test GPT-4 (announced March 14, 2023) [38] and GPT-3.5 Turbo (announced March 1, 2023) [11]. From Anthropic, we test Claude v1.3 [5] (announced April 18, 2023). Both GPT-4 and Claude-v1.3 have undergone extensive safety training that make them challenging to attack naïvely: OpenAI [38] reports that GPT-4 responds to requests for disallowed content 82% less than GPT-3.5, and Anthropic states Claude v1.3 is “safer and less susceptible to adversarial attacks” [5].
paras.zh: 我们评估了在能力和安全性方面均处于最先进水平的语言模型。来自 OpenAI，我们测试了 GPT-4（2023 年 3 月 14 日发布）[38] 和 GPT-3.5 Turbo（2023 年 3 月 1 日发布）[11]。来自 Anthropic，我们测试了 Claude v1.3 [5]（2023 年 4 月 18 日发布）。GPT-4 和 Claude-v1.3 都经过了广泛的安全训练，使得它们难以被简单攻击：OpenAI [38] 报告称，GPT-4 对不允许内容的请求响应比 GPT-3.5 减少了 82%，而 Anthropic 表示 Claude v1.3 “更安全且不易受到对抗性攻击” [5]。

paras.en: To minimize the impact of incremental model updates, we collect data for each model over a 6-day window (details in Section C.1). To minimize the impact of noise from decoding, we sample with temperature 0. (As a robustness check, we test sampling with temperature 111 in Appendix G and find that the best attacks remain effective.) For the GPT models, which have a system prompt, we use the suggested system prompt “You are a helpful assistant.” [39].
paras.zh: 为了最小化增量模型更新的影响，我们在 6 天的时间窗口内为每个模型收集数据（详见 C.1 节）。为了最小化解码噪声的影响，我们使用温度为 0 进行采样（作为稳健性检查，我们在附录 G 中测试了温度为 1 的采样，发现最佳攻击仍然有效）。对于具有系统提示的 GPT 模型，我们使用建议的系统提示“你是一个有用的助手。” [39]。

paras.en: We evaluate models and jailbreaks using two datasets of harmful prompts: a curated set of 32 harmful prompts from the OpenAI and Anthropic red teaming and a larger, held-out set of 317 harmful prompts generated by GPT-4, constructed following Shaikh et al. [44]. Due to query and annotation cost, we only evaluate top-performing attacks on the larger dataset to demonstrate the generalizability of the attacks. We summarize the datasets here and give further details in Appendix A.
paras.zh: 我们使用两个有害提示数据集评估模型和越狱攻击：一个来自 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 红队测试的 32 个有害提示的精选集，以及一个更大的、由 GPT-4 生成的 317 个有害提示的保留集，按照 Shaikh 等人 [44] 构建。由于查询和标注成本，我们仅在更大的数据集上评估表现最好的攻击，以证明攻击的泛化性。我们在此总结数据集，并在附录 A 中提供更多细节。

paras.en: The curated dataset consists of all 16 examples of harmful prompts used to evaluate GPT-4 from its report [38] and 16 harmful prompts adapted222The red-teaming dataset consists of dialogue transcripts that must be adapted to obtain standalone prompts. from the red-teaming dataset of Ganguli et al. [23] to ensure coverage of each of their 17 harmful prompt tags. Selecting from red team efforts (i) ensures the prompts ask for behaviors deemed harmful by the model creators and (ii) presents a challenging target for attack, as such examples were used to inform safety training. The user request in Figure 1 is an example of a prompt (of mild nature) from this dataset.
paras.zh: 精选数据集包括其报告 [38] 中用于评估 GPT-4 的所有 16 个有害提示示例，以及从 Ganguli 等人 [23] 的红队测试数据集中改编的 16 个有害提示，以确保覆盖其 17 个有害提示标签中的每一个。从红队测试中选择（i）确保提示要求模型创建者认为有害的行为，并且（ii）为攻击提供了具有挑战性的目标，因为此类示例曾用于指导安全训练。图 1 中的用户请求是该数据集中一个提示（性质温和）的示例。

paras.en: The larger dataset of 317 prompts was constructed following Shaikh et al. [44], based on few-shot sampling from GPT-4. As a proxy for restricted behavior, the dataset was further filtered to consist only of prompts that neither GPT-4 nor Claude v1.3 would respond to. To maximize statistical validity, the dataset was not used to design attacks and was not seen by the authors until after data collection.
paras.zh: 包含 317 个提示的更大数据集是按照 Shaikh 等人 [44] 的方法，基于从 GPT-4 进行少样本采样构建的。作为受限行为的代理，该数据集进一步过滤，仅包含 GPT-4 和 Claude v1.3 都不会响应的提示。为了最大化统计有效性，该数据集未用于设计攻击，并且在数据收集完成之前作者未见过。

paras.en: Beyond harm, we also evaluate jailbreaks on inducing PII leakage and hallucination in Appendix H.
paras.zh: 除了危害之外，我们还在附录 H 中评估了越狱攻击在诱导 PII 泄露和幻觉方面的效果。

full.sec: 3 Failure Modes: Competing Objectives and Generalization Mismatch
full.secZh: 3 失败模式：竞争性目标与泛化失配

paras.en: To understand the vulnerability of safety-trained LLMs to jailbreak attacks, it is essential to identify the failure modes of safety training that underlie this vulnerability. We propose two failure modes that can be traced down to the level of data and training: (i) competition between the capabilities and safety objectives, and (ii) mismatched generalization between the pretraining and safety capabilities. Beyond offering a hypothesis for why jailbreaks work, we functionally validate these failure modes by using them as principles to construct new jailbreaks.
paras.zh: 要理解经过安全训练的大型语言模型对越狱攻击的脆弱性，必须识别出导致这种脆弱性的安全训练失败模式。我们提出了两种可以追溯到数据和训练层面的失败模式：(i) 能力目标与安全目标之间的竞争，以及 (ii) 预训练能力与安全能力之间的泛化失配。除了为越狱为何有效提供假设外，我们还通过将这些失败模式作为构建新越狱的原则来对其进行功能验证。

paras.en: In this section, we explore each failure mode with qualitative examples, construct simple jailbreaks that illustrate their mechanisms of action, and describe ablations to test our claims. We later validate these insights with quantitative experiments in Section 4.
paras.zh: 在本节中，我们通过定性示例探讨每种失败模式，构建简单的越狱来说明其作用机制，并进行消融实验来检验我们的主张。我们将在第4节中通过定量实验进一步验证这些见解。

paras.en: Competing objectives stems from the observation that safety-trained LLMs are typically trained against multiple objectives that can conflict with each other. Specifically, state-of-the-art LLMs are trained for language modeling [12], instruction following [41, 7], and safety [38, 7]. This training can be exploited by crafting prompts that force a choice between either a restricted behavior or a response that is heavily penalized by the pretraining and instruction following objectives.
paras.zh: 竞争目标源于观察到经过安全训练的大语言模型通常针对多个可能相互冲突的目标进行训练。具体来说，最先进的大语言模型针对语言建模 [12]、指令遵循 [41, 7] 和安全 [38, 7] 进行训练。这种训练可以通过精心设计的提示来利用，迫使模型在受限行为或受到预训练和指令遵循目标严重惩罚的响应之间做出选择。

paras.en: To illustrate competing objectives, we introduce a family of prefix injection jailbreaks. This attack asks the model to first output an innocuous-looking prefix designed so that conditioning on the prefix makes refusal unlikely in the pretraining distribution. The attack in Figure 1(a) uses the prefix “Absolutely! Here’s”. Another example, with prefix in purple, looks like:
paras.zh: 为了说明竞争目标，我们引入了一系列前缀注入越狱攻击。这种攻击要求模型首先输出一个看似无害的前缀，该前缀的设计使得在预训练分布中，基于该前缀的条件拒绝不太可能发生。图1(a)中的攻击使用了前缀“Absolutely! Here’s”。另一个例子，前缀为紫色，如下所示：

paras.en: The above attack can lead GPT-4 to provide harmful information on harassment, crime, and violence. As an ablation, we show the injected prefix text matters: changing the prefix to “Hello!” makes GPT-4 no longer exhibit the aforementioned behaviors. (See Section 4 for the quantitative results.)
paras.zh: 上述攻击可能导致 GPT-4 提供关于骚扰、犯罪和暴力的有害信息。作为消融实验，我们表明注入的前缀文本很重要：将前缀改为“Hello!”会使 GPT-4 不再表现出上述行为。（定量结果见第4节。）

paras.en: When an LLM decodes a response to this prompt, we hypothesize that this attack exploits competing objectives in two ways: First, the harmless-looking injection instruction is followed, since models are penalized for refusing harmless instructions [7, 38]. Then, since it would be unlikely to see a refusal after the prefix in the pretraining distribution, the model’s pretraining objective heavily penalizes refusing. As a result, the model continues with a response to the unsafe prompt.
paras.zh: 当大语言模型解码对此提示的响应时，我们假设这种攻击以两种方式利用竞争目标：首先，无害的注入指令被遵循，因为模型因拒绝无害指令而受到惩罚 [7, 38]。然后，由于在预训练分布中，前缀后出现拒绝的可能性很小，模型的预训练目标会严重惩罚拒绝。结果，模型继续对不安全提示做出响应。

paras.en: We introduce refusal suppression as a second family of jailbreaks from competing objectives, to highlight how instruction following can play a primary role. In this attack, the model is instructed to respond under constraints that rule out common refusal responses, thus making unsafe responses more likely. An example refusal suppression jailbreak looks like:
paras.zh: 我们引入拒绝抑制作为竞争目标的第二类越狱攻击，以强调指令遵循如何发挥主要作用。在这种攻击中，模型被指示在排除常见拒绝响应的约束下做出响应，从而使不安全响应更有可能发生。一个拒绝抑制越狱的例子如下所示：

paras.en: The above attack leads GPT-4 to respond to prompts asking for advice about crime, social engineering, and adult content, among others. In an ablation experiment, we find that the specific instructions matter: inverting the rules (e.g., “1. Consider apologizing …”) leads to no restricted behavior on any prompt in the dataset. (See Section 4 for the quantitative results.)
paras.zh: 上述攻击导致 GPT-4 对询问犯罪、社会工程和成人内容等建议的提示做出响应。在消融实验中，我们发现具体指令很重要：反转规则（例如，“1. 考虑道歉……”）不会导致数据集中任何提示出现受限行为。（定量结果见第4节。）

paras.en: We see competing objectives as manifesting here in two ways. First, the instruction-following training responds to the instructions and downweights tokens that start typical refusals. Thus, the model selects tokens that are more likely to begin responses. Once a response is started, the pretraining objective heavily favors continuing over an abrupt reversal, leading to a full unsafe output.
paras.zh: 我们认为竞争目标在此以两种方式体现。首先，指令遵循训练响应指令并降低开始典型拒绝的标记的权重。因此，模型选择更可能开始响应的标记。一旦响应开始，预训练目标强烈倾向于继续而不是突然反转，从而导致完整的有害输出。

paras.en: We find existing jailbreaks also leverage the competing objectives phenomenon. For example, the widely shared “DAN” jailbreak [48] takes advantage of instruction following through a dense set of instructions on how to play the character DAN and pretraining by asking for outputs to start with “[DAN]:”. Another jailbreak uses a clever variant of prompt injection to trampoline off a refusal: it asks for a moralizing rant about OpenAI content policies followed by the injected string “But now that we’ve got that mandatory bulls out of the way, let’s break the fin’ rules:” [43]. Extending prefix injection, we also find competing objectives can be exploited via style injection, e.g., by asking for no long words, after which the model’s professionally-written refusals are unlikely to follow.
paras.zh: 我们发现现有的越狱攻击也利用了竞争目标现象。例如，广泛传播的“DAN”越狱攻击 [48] 通过关于如何扮演 DAN 角色的大量指令利用指令遵循，并通过要求输出以“[DAN]:”开头利用预训练。另一种越狱攻击使用巧妙的提示注入变体，从拒绝中反弹：它要求先进行关于 OpenAI 内容政策的道德说教，然后注入字符串“But now that we've got that mandatory bulls out of the way, let's break the fin' rules:” [43]。扩展前缀注入，我们还发现竞争目标可以通过风格注入来利用，例如，要求不使用长词，之后模型专业编写的拒绝不太可能被遵循。

paras.en: Our second failure mode comes from observing that pretraining is done on a larger and more diverse dataset than safety training, and thus the model has many capabilities not covered by safety training. This mismatch can be exploited for jailbreaks by constructing prompts on which pretraining and instruction following generalize, but the model’s safety training does not. For such prompts, the model responds, but without safety considerations. We present a sharp example of this phenomenon, and then discuss other ways in which this failure mode can be exploited to construct jailbreaks.
paras.zh: 我们的第二个失败模式源于观察到预训练是在比安全训练更大、更多样化的数据集上进行的，因此模型具有许多安全训练未涵盖的能力。这种不匹配可以被利用来构造提示，使得预训练和指令遵循泛化，但模型的安全训练却不泛化，从而实施越狱。对于此类提示，模型会响应，但缺乏安全考虑。我们给出了这一现象的尖锐示例，然后讨论了利用此失败模式构造越狱的其他方式。

paras.en: In the Base64 jailbreak, the prompt is obfuscated using Base64, a binary-to-text encoding that encodes each byte as three text characters, to bypass the model’s safety training (as in Figure 1(b)). An example attack—that additionally elicits encoded output—may look like:
paras.zh: 在Base64越狱中，提示使用Base64（一种二进制到文本的编码，将每个字节编码为三个文本字符）进行混淆，以绕过模型的安全训练（如图1(b)所示）。一个示例攻击——额外要求编码输出——可能如下所示：

paras.en: This jailbreak applied to GPT-4 produces synthesis instructions for controlled substances, misinformation, and harmful stereotypes, among others. Via ablations, we find encoded input to be more important than encoded output, although the combination is by far the most potent. (That encoded output helps shows that the model’s partially decoded prefix guides refusal behavior, a property also exploited by prefix injection.)
paras.zh: 将此越狱应用于GPT-4会产生受控物质的合成说明、错误信息和有害刻板印象等。通过消融实验，我们发现编码输入比编码输出更重要，尽管组合是最有效的。（编码输出有帮助表明模型的部分解码前缀引导了拒绝行为，这也是前缀注入所利用的属性。）

paras.en: Mismatched generalization likely occurs because large models (e.g., GPT-4 and Claude v1.3) pick up Base64 during pretraining and learn to directly follow Base64-encoded instructions. On the other hand, it is also likely that safety training does not contain inputs that are as unnatural as Base64-encoded instructions, so the model has never been trained to refuse such prompts. It is thus plausible that the model fails to respond with a refusal because the input is far out-of-distribution. Furthermore, while safety training is claimed to generalize across languages [41, 6], the success of this attack suggests caution in how far off distribution one should expect safety training to extend.
paras.zh: 不匹配的泛化很可能发生，因为大型模型（例如GPT-4和Claude v1.3）在预训练中学习了Base64，并学会了直接遵循Base64编码的指令。另一方面，安全训练很可能不包含像Base64编码指令那样不自然的输入，因此模型从未被训练拒绝此类提示。因此，模型未能拒绝响应很可能是由于输入严重偏离分布。此外，尽管安全训练声称能跨语言泛化[41, 6]，但此攻击的成功表明，在期望安全训练扩展到多远分布外的问题上应保持谨慎。

paras.en: There is a vast space of obfuscation schemes: At the character-level, they include the ROT13 cipher, leetspeak (replacing letters with visually similar numbers and symbols), and Morse code [8]. At the word-level, they include Pig Latin, replacing sensitive words with synonyms (e.g., “pilfer” instead of “steal”), or payload splitting [30] (a.k.a. “token smuggling” [37]) to split sensitive words into substrings. Prompt-level obfuscations include translation to other languages or just asking the model to obfuscate in a way that it can understand [53]. In many such instances, the model can still follow the obfuscated instructions, but safety fails to transfer.
paras.zh: 存在大量的混淆方案：在字符级别，包括ROT13密码、leet语（用视觉上相似的数字和符号替换字母）和摩尔斯电码[8]。在单词级别，包括猪拉丁语、用同义词替换敏感词（例如用“pilfer”代替“steal”），或有效载荷分割[30]（又称“令牌走私”[37]）将敏感词拆分为子串。提示级别的混淆包括翻译成其他语言，或仅要求模型以它能理解的方式混淆[53]。在许多此类实例中，模型仍然可以遵循混淆后的指令，但安全性未能迁移。

paras.en: Going beyond obfuscation, LLMs have many other capabilities not explored during safety training. Other ways in which pretraining and instruction following generalize, but safety does not, include: (i) “distractor” instructions, i.e., many random requests written in a row; (ii) asking for responses with unusual output formats (e.g., JSON); (iii) asking for content from a website the model would have seen during pretraining but not mentioned during safety training, e.g.,
paras.zh: 超越混淆，LLM还有许多安全训练未探索的其他能力。预训练和指令遵循泛化但安全性不泛化的其他方式包括：(i) “干扰”指令，即连续多个随机请求；(ii) 要求以不寻常的输出格式（例如JSON）响应；(iii) 要求提供模型在预训练中见过但安全训练中未提及的网站内容，例如

paras.en: We now quantitatively evaluate jailbreak methods on GPT-4, Claude v1.3, and the smaller GPT-3.5 Turbo across combinations of harmful prompts and attacks to understand the vulnerability landscape of these models. Our results confirm the analyses of Section 3, highlight the diversity of jailbreaks that can work, reveal that combinations of simple ideas yield the strongest jailbreaks, and demonstrate that the strongest jailbreaks successfully attack almost all prompts for these models.
paras.zh: 我们现在对GPT-4、Claude v1.3以及较小的GPT-3.5 Turbo进行越狱方法的定量评估，涵盖有害提示和攻击的组合，以了解这些模型的脆弱性全貌。我们的结果证实了第3节的分析，突出了可奏效的越狱方法的多样性，揭示了简单想法的组合能产生最强的越狱效果，并证明了最强的越狱方法能成功攻击这些模型的几乎所有提示。

paras.en: We evaluate 30 jailbreak methods, primarily constructed based on the principles in Section 3. Several of these attacks also have variations appearing in the public discourse. We summarize the attacks here and provide full details in Section C.2.
paras.zh: 我们评估了30种越狱方法，主要基于第3节中的原理构建。其中一些攻击在公开讨论中也有变体。我们在此总结这些攻击，并在第C.2节中提供完整细节。

paras.en: As a control, we test a none jailbreak that simply echoes each prompt verbatim.
paras.zh: 作为对照，我们测试了一种无越狱方法，即简单地逐字重复每个提示。

paras.en: We test a number of simple attacks involving ideas based on competing objectives and mismatched generalization, including prefix injection, refusal suppression, Base64 encoding, style injection, distractor instructions, other obfuscations, and generating website content (Wikipedia).
paras.zh: 我们测试了若干基于竞争目标和失配泛化思想的简单攻击，包括前缀注入、拒绝抑制、Base64编码、风格注入、干扰指令、其他混淆以及生成网站内容（维基百科）。

paras.en: We also test combinations of these basic attack techniques: combination_1 composes prefix injection, refusal suppression, and the Base64 attack, combination_2 adds style injection, and combination_3 adds generating website content and formatting constraints.
paras.zh: 我们还测试了这些基本攻击技术的组合：combination_1 组合了前缀注入、拒绝抑制和Base64攻击，combination_2 增加了风格注入，combination_3 增加了生成网站内容和格式约束。

paras.en: We explore using LLMs to streamline jailbreak attacks by considering two model-assisted attacks: auto_payload_splitting asks GPT-4 to flag sensitive phrases to obfuscate, while auto_obfuscation uses the LLM to generate an arbitrary obfuscation of the prompt.
paras.zh: 我们探索使用LLM来简化越狱攻击，考虑了两种模型辅助攻击：auto_payload_splitting 要求GPT-4标记敏感短语以进行混淆，而auto_obfuscation 使用LLM生成提示的任意混淆。

paras.en: We include four attacks from the jailbreak sharing site jailbreakchat.com [2]. To select the best popular jailbreaks, we chose the top two attacks on April 13, 2023 each in terms of “Votes” and “JB score” [3]. These attacks are similar in spirit to DAN [48], centering around role play while leveraging competing objectives through detailed instructions and prefix injection.
paras.zh: 我们包含了来自越狱分享网站 jailbreakchat.com [2] 的四种攻击。为了选择最佳流行越狱，我们选取了2023年4月13日“投票数”和“JB分数”[3]各自排名前两位的攻击。这些攻击在精神上与DAN [48]类似，围绕角色扮演，通过详细指令和前缀注入利用竞争目标。

paras.en: As an additional comparison, we evaluate GPT models on a system prompt attack as described in the GPT-4 technical report [38]. (Claude does not have an analogous system prompt.) We set the system prompt to be the Evil Confidant attack from jailbreakchat.com. Note, however, that this attack is technically beyond the scope of our threat model in Section 2.1.
paras.zh: 作为额外比较，我们在GPT模型上评估了GPT-4技术报告[38]中描述的系统提示攻击。（Claude没有类似的系统提示。）我们将系统提示设置为来自 jailbreakchat.com 的Evil Confidant攻击。但请注意，此攻击在技术上超出了我们第2.1节威胁模型的范围。

paras.en: To simulate an adaptive adversary who can choose the attack based on the prompt, we consider a simple “adaptive” attack that succeeds if any of the 28 evaluated attacks succeed.
paras.zh: 为了模拟能够根据提示选择攻击的自适应对手，我们考虑了一种简单的“自适应”攻击：如果28种评估攻击中的任何一种成功，则该攻击成功。

paras.en: We evaluate jailbreaks on GPT-4, Claude v1.3, and GPT-3.5 Turbo against the datasets of harmful prompts introduced in Section 2.2. In the first phase, we test each jailbreak for each model against the curated dataset and an additional harmless control prompt. In the second phase, we perform a concentrated evaluation of the top three attacks against the dataset of 317 prompts, for both GPT-4 and Claude v1.3. For each phase, the authors manually labeled the resulting model outputs following the scheme in Appendix B.333We evaluate results by hand as many outputs can be obfuscated or encoded with errors. To ensure consistency, we exactly follow the labeling scheme specified in Appendix B. In total, we process 2,970 samples for the curated dataset and 2,536 samples for the synthetic dataset. We report results as the fractions of outcomes that were Good Bot\xspace, Bad Bot\xspace, and Unclear\xspace.
paras.zh: 我们在GPT-4、Claude v1.3和GPT-3.5 Turbo上评估越狱攻击，针对第2.2节引入的有害提示数据集。在第一阶段，我们针对每个模型，在精选数据集和一个额外的无害控制提示上测试每种越狱攻击。在第二阶段，我们对GPT-4和Claude v1.3的前三种攻击在317个提示的数据集上进行集中评估。每个阶段，作者按照附录B中的方案手动标记模型输出。333我们手动评估结果，因为许多输出可能被混淆或带有编码错误。为确保一致性，我们严格遵循附录B中指定的标记方案。总共，我们处理了精选数据集的2,970个样本和合成数据集的2,536个样本。我们报告结果为“好机器人”、“坏机器人”和“不明确”结果的占比。

paras.en: A quick inspection of Table 1 reveals that a variety of jailbreak attacks have traction on these models, suggesting that the space of successful jailbreaks can be vast. And while individual simple attacks succeed only on a fraction of the prompts, their combinations in the combination_* attacks are extremely effective. The top jailbreakchat.com prompt AIM is also a combination attack. This suggests that combinations of simple attacks—of which there can be combinatorially many—may be the most difficult to defend against. We also verify that the control jailbreak none has a very low Bad Bot\xspacerate, further confirming that these prompts are indeed unsafe.
paras.zh: 快速查看表1可以发现，多种越狱攻击对这些模型有效，表明成功的越狱空间可能非常广阔。虽然单个简单攻击仅对部分提示成功，但它们在组合攻击（combination_*）中的组合却极为有效。排名靠前的 jailbreakchat.com 提示 AIM 也是一种组合攻击。这表明简单攻击的组合——其数量可能是组合爆炸的——可能是最难防御的。我们还验证了控制越狱（none）的恶意机器人率非常低，进一步证实这些提示确实不安全。

paras.en: Examining the performance of the adaptive attack across Tables 1, 2 and 3, we see that, for any given prompt, at least one of the tested jailbreaks succeeds almost 100% of the time. Thus, it is likely that a motivated attacker could elicit restricted behavior from these models on many other unsafe prompts with only minor variations of the jailbreaks we investigate in this work.
paras.zh: 检查表1、表2和表3中自适应攻击的表现，我们发现，对于任何给定的提示，至少有一种测试的越狱几乎100%成功。因此，一个动机明确的攻击者很可能仅通过对我们研究中调查的越狱进行微小变体，就能在这些模型上引发受限行为，针对许多其他不安全提示。

paras.en: On defense, our results suggest that targeted training is insufficient: There is evidence that Claude v1.3 was trained to refuse harmful role play [23, 4]. Indeed, all roleplay attacks have 0% success rate, including the jailbreakchat.com attacks that succeed on GPT-4. (Claude even refuses a harmless control prompt under these roleplay attacks; see Appendix D.) Yet Claude v1.3 remains vulnerable to other attack strategies and is 100% vulnerable to an adaptive attack.
paras.zh: 在防御方面，我们的结果表明有针对性的训练是不够的：有证据表明 Claude v1.3 经过训练以拒绝有害角色扮演[23, 4]。确实，所有角色扮演攻击的成功率为0%，包括在 GPT-4 上成功的 jailbreakchat.com 攻击。（Claude 甚至在这些角色扮演攻击下拒绝无害的控制提示；见附录D。）然而，Claude v1.3 仍然容易受到其他攻击策略的影响，并且对自适应攻击100%脆弱。

paras.en: Finally, Table 3 reveals that scale can shift the attack surface and introduce new vulnerabilities. The roleplay attacks and the system prompt attack are much more effective on GPT-3.5 Turbo than GPT-4. On the other hand, more complex attacks like combination_* and auto_payload_splitting do not work on GPT-3.5 Turbo. We identify this as GPT-3.5 Turbo not having the capability to understand complex inputs: evidence comes from the Base64 examples being Unclear\xspaceat a high rate and the harmless control prompts not succeeding (see Figure 2 and Appendix D). This suggests that certain jailbreak vulnerabilities only emerge at sufficient scale.
paras.zh: 最后，表3显示规模可以改变攻击面并引入新的漏洞。角色扮演攻击和系统提示攻击在 GPT-3.5 Turbo 上比在 GPT-4 上有效得多。另一方面，更复杂的攻击如组合攻击（combination_*）和自动载荷分割（auto_payload_splitting）在 GPT-3.5 Turbo 上不起作用。我们将其归因于 GPT-3.5 Turbo 不具备理解复杂输入的能力：证据来自 Base64 示例的“不明确”率高以及无害控制提示不成功（见图2和附录D）。这表明某些越狱漏洞仅在足够规模下才会出现。

paras.en: We now discuss the implications of our findings for defense. We argue that (i) scaling alone will not resolve the failure modes of Section 3, and (ii) “safety-capability parity”—where safety mechanisms match the sophistication of the base model—may be necessary to defend against adversarial use.
paras.zh: 我们现在讨论我们的发现对防御的启示。我们认为，(i) 仅靠 Scaling（规模扩张）无法解决第 3 节中的失效模式，并且 (ii) “安全-能力对等”——即安全机制与基础模型的复杂程度相匹配——可能是防御对抗性使用所必需的。

paras.en: To see the limitations of scaling, consider first the competing objectives failure mode. The root cause of this failure mode is likely the optimization objective rather than the dataset or model size. Take, for instance, the RLHF objective of InstructGPT [41], on which GPT-4 is based. It includes terms for KL divergence from the base model and loss on the pretraining distribution. Thus, even during safety training, trading off between safety and pretraining is inherent, leaving the model vulnerable to choosing pretraining over safety. This is further evidenced by the same attack principles working on GPT-4 as GPT-3, even if specific prompts require modification. To fully resolve the issue of competing objectives, one may have to move beyond the pretrain-then-finetune paradigm and, e.g., incorporate human values starting from pretraining [31].
paras.zh: 要理解 Scaling（规模扩张）的局限性，首先考虑竞争目标失效模式。该失效模式的根源很可能是优化目标，而非数据集或模型大小。例如，以 GPT-4 所基于的 InstructGPT [41] 的 RLHF（基于人类反馈的强化学习）目标为例。它包含与基础模型的 KL 散度以及预训练分布上的损失项。因此，即使在安全训练期间，安全与预训练之间的权衡也是固有的，使得模型容易选择预训练而非安全。这一点进一步由相同的攻击原理在 GPT-4 和 GPT-3 上均有效所证明，即使特定提示需要修改。要完全解决竞争目标问题，可能需要超越预训练-再微调范式，例如，从预训练开始就融入人类价值观 [31]。

paras.en: Mismatched generalization is also not resolved by scaling alone, as more data and larger models will not guarantee that safety training generalizes as broadly as model capabilities. In fact, we find that scale can exacerbate instruction-following finetuning generalizing better than safety finetuning: GPT-3.5 Turbo cannot follow Base64-encoded instructions (Figure 2 (left) and Table 3). However, GPT-4 can follow Base64-encoded instructions, but with fewer safeguards (Figure 2 (right) and Table 1). As scale increases further, the set of model capabilities will continue to expand (e.g., GPT-4 cannot reliably follow instructions in ROT13, but GPT-5 might be able to do so). Thus, scaling may lead to a combinatorially growing attack surface of capabilities to defend.
paras.zh: 不匹配泛化也不能仅靠 Scaling（规模扩张）解决，因为更多的数据和更大的模型并不能保证安全训练能像模型能力那样广泛地泛化。事实上，我们发现规模会加剧指令遵循微调比安全微调泛化得更好：GPT-3.5 Turbo 无法遵循 Base64 编码的指令（图 2（左）和表 3）。然而，GPT-4 可以遵循 Base64 编码的指令，但安全保障较少（图 2（右）和表 1）。随着规模进一步增大，模型能力集将继续扩展（例如，GPT-4 无法可靠地遵循 ROT13 编码的指令，但 GPT-5 可能能够做到）。因此，Scaling（规模扩张）可能导致需要防御的能力攻击面呈组合式增长。

paras.en: Our findings also suggest the necessity of “safety-capability parity”—where safety mechanisms are as sophisticated as the underlying model. Otherwise, attacks will exploit cutting-edge capabilities of the model that less advanced safety mechanisms cannot detect or address. For instance, flagging and filtering by a less capable model are not robust solutions because they may fail to recognize threats: a model without Base64 decoding ability would not be able to flag the Base64-encoded inputs and outputs of the Base64 attack. Even sophisticated human labelers may struggle to evaluate obfuscated and adversarial inputs and outputs without assistance. This asymmetry will only grow with scale, as more capable language models might be capable of even more subtle forms of output (e.g., steganography) that would further evade detection.
paras.zh: 我们的发现还表明“安全-能力对等”的必要性——即安全机制与底层模型一样复杂。否则，攻击将利用模型的前沿能力，而较不先进的安全机制无法检测或应对这些能力。例如，由能力较弱的模型进行标记和过滤并非稳健的解决方案，因为它们可能无法识别威胁：不具备 Base64 解码能力的模型将无法标记 Base64 攻击的 Base64 编码输入和输出。即使是复杂的人类标注员，在没有帮助的情况下也可能难以评估混淆和对抗性输入及输出。这种不对称性将随着规模增长而加剧，因为能力更强的语言模型可能能够产生更微妙的输出形式（例如隐写术），从而进一步逃避检测。

paras.en: Moreover, we anticipate an arms race where models can be leveraged to generate attacks that defeat their own safety mechanisms; indeed, some of our own attacks had portions automated by LLMs. For instance, the auto_payload_splitting attack used GPT-4 to flag words that are sensitive in context. With the following prompt, the phrases “cut down” and “stop sign” are flagged:
paras.zh: 此外，我们预计会出现一场军备竞赛，其中模型可被用来生成击败自身安全机制的攻击；事实上，我们的一些攻击部分是由 LLM 自动完成的。例如，auto_payload_splitting 攻击使用 GPT-4 来标记在上下文中敏感的词语。通过以下提示，短语“cut down”和“stop sign”被标记：

paras.en: And the auto_obfuscation attack successfully identified new “languages” that the models could understand: Claude rediscovered the Base64 attack, while GPT-4 uncovered leetspeak in Spanish—see Appendix F for details. Since LLM capabilities can emerge unpredictably [51], new capabilities can be difficult to anticipate and prepare for. Thus, to have complete coverage of the attack surface, future models will likely need to at least be safeguarded by models of similar sophistication.
paras.zh: 而 auto_obfuscation 攻击成功识别了模型能够理解的新的“语言”：Claude 重新发现了 Base64 攻击，而 GPT-4 发现了西班牙语中的 leetspeak——详见附录 F。由于 LLM 能力可能不可预测地涌现 [51]，新能力可能难以预测和准备。因此，为了完全覆盖攻击面，未来的模型可能至少需要由类似复杂程度的模型来保护。

paras.en: While safety training can make LLMs less likely to demonstrate undesirable behavior under normal use, existing methods are ineffective against adversarial actors. In this paper, we hypothesize conceptual failure modes of LLM safety training and demonstrate that they yield principles for crafting effective jailbreak attacks. In particular, our investigation highlights that such methods often fail to be safe by design [16]: that even their idealized execution still leads to exploitable vulnerabilities, with issues that cannot be fixed by more data and scale.
paras.zh: 虽然安全训练可以使大语言模型在正常使用下不太可能表现出不良行为，但现有方法对对抗性行为者无效。在本文中，我们假设了LLM安全训练的概念性失效模式，并证明它们产生了构建有效越狱攻击的原则。特别是，我们的研究强调，这些方法往往未能实现设计上的安全[16]：即使其理想化的执行仍然会导致可利用的漏洞，且这些问题无法通过更多数据和规模来解决。

paras.en: We view this work as an early exploration of the robustness of safety-trained language models. As such, much remains to be done. Due to the proprietary nature of state-of-the-art LLMs like GPT-4 and Claude, we are limited to indirect confirmation of our hypotheses. This highlights the need for open research replications of safety-trained models to enable detailed study. Future research may seek to understand whether the results of safety training can be mechanistically interpreted [36] and whether more potent jailbreaks can be devised with white-box access. Open questions remain about black-box jailbreaks as well, such as the potential for automated discovery and patching of jailbreaks and the effectiveness of multi-round interactions in jailbreak attacks.
paras.zh: 我们将这项工作视为对安全训练语言模型鲁棒性的早期探索。因此，还有许多工作要做。由于GPT-4和Claude等最先进LLM的专有性质，我们只能间接确认我们的假设。这凸显了需要对安全训练模型进行开放研究复现，以便进行详细研究。未来的研究可能试图理解安全训练的结果是否可以被机制性地解释[36]，以及是否可以通过白盒访问设计出更强大的越狱攻击。关于黑盒越狱也存在未解决的问题，例如越狱攻击的自动发现和修补的潜力，以及多轮交互在越狱攻击中的有效性。

paras.en: We recognize that our investigation into the vulnerabilities of safety-trained LLMs has the potential for misuse. To mitigate this risk, we have adhered to responsible disclosure practices by sharing our preliminary findings with OpenAI and Anthropic prior to submission. We further coordinated with them before publicly releasing our results. We also emphasize that, as our ultimate goal in this paper is to identify of weaknesses of existing methods rather than create new jailbreak attacks, our presentation centers around the conceptual aspects instead of details of attacks.
paras.zh: 我们认识到，我们对安全训练LLM漏洞的研究存在被滥用的可能。为减轻这一风险，我们遵循了负责任的披露实践，在提交前与OpenAI和Anthropic分享了我们的初步发现。我们还在公开发布结果前与他们进行了协调。我们同时强调，由于本文的最终目标是识别现有方法的弱点而非创造新的越狱攻击，我们的呈现侧重于概念方面而非攻击细节。

paras.en: Finally, we believe that open discussion of weaknesses and limitations is vital for the development of robust future systems. As LLM-based systems become more prevalent, it is essential to understand their safety and how they might be exploited: the stakes for these systems will only increase as they move beyond the chatbox and into the real world. With this in mind, we hope our work sheds light on some of the challenges faced by existing methods and facilitates future research into the safe and reliable deployment of LLMs.
paras.zh: 最后，我们相信，对弱点和局限性的公开讨论对于开发稳健的未来系统至关重要。随着基于LLM的系统变得越来越普遍，理解其安全性以及它们可能如何被利用至关重要：随着这些系统超越聊天机器人进入现实世界，其风险只会增加。考虑到这一点，我们希望我们的工作能揭示现有方法面临的一些挑战，并促进未来关于LLM安全可靠部署的研究。
